entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: An essential nutrient for plant growth and development

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS ACQUISITION
entities.description: The ability of crop plants to take up phosphorus from the soil

entities.description: Phosphorus in the soil that is not readily available for plant uptake

entities.name: ROCK PHOSPHATE
entities.description: A mineral source of phosphorus used as fertilizer or amendment

entities.name: GENETIC MANIPULATION
entities.description: The process of altering the genetic makeup of a plant to achieve desired traits

entities.name: INTEGRATED NUTRIENT MANAGEMENT
entities.description: A holistic approach to managing nutrients in cropping systems

entities.name: ROOT ABSORPTION AREA
entities.description: The total surface area of the root system available for nutrient uptake

entities.name: ROOTING CHARACTERISTICS
entities.description: Physical properties and growth patterns of the root system

entities.name: MYCORRHIZAL ASSOCIATIONS
entities.description: Symbiotic relationships between fungi and plant roots

entities.name: ION ABSORPTION MECHANISMS
entities.description: Biological processes by which plants take up ions from the soil

entities.name: RHIZOSPHERE
entities.description: The narrow region of soil that is influenced by root secretions and associated soil microorganisms

entities.name: ROOT EXUDATIONS
entities.description: Chemical substances secreted by plant roots into the rhizosphere

entities.name: MICROORGANISMS
entities.description: Microscopic organisms in the soil that affect nutrient availability

entities.name: ROOT MORPHOLOGY
entities.description: The physical structure and form of the root system

entities.name: CROP MANAGEMENT PRACTICES
entities.description: Agricultural techniques used to manage crops and their environment

entities.name: CROPPING SYSTEMS APPROACHES
entities.description: Strategies for the spatial and temporal arrangement of crops
